The approximate energy costs of various physical activities in different intensities for a 60 kg person are given in annexure-11. If a physical activity does not increase the heart rate, it is not intense enough to be counted in the category of “45 minutes of exercise a day”. Activities that do not increase the heart rate include walking at a casual pace, grocery shopping, and doing light household chores. These activities are very beneficial to health. These speed your heart rate and breathing while improving heart and lung fitness. Examples: brisk walking, jogging and swimming.
